issuesFound.title: mobileNav.toggle does not update aria-expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle opens and closes the drawer but never sets or updates the aria-expanded attribute on the hamburger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the drawer’s state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when closing the drawer.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger elements lack initial aria-expanded="false"
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is initialized its trigger button does not have aria‑expanded set to false, so screen readers cannot know that the menu is collapsed initially.
issuesFound.fix: In `register`, after ensuring aria‑haspopup, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu hide does not set aria-hidden on the panel
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is closed the code only adds/removes CSS classes and the hidden attribute, but never sets `aria-hidden="true"` on the panel, leaving it perceivable to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In `_closeInstance`, after hiding the panel, call `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `_openInstance`, remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm validation errors are not reflected with aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form uses `inlineValidator` which adds error messages but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on invalid inputs, so assistive technologies cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In `inlineValidator.validate`, after a field fails validation, call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; on success, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: contactForm validation errors are not reflected with aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form also relies on `inlineValidator` and suffers the same missing `aria-invalid` attribute on invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 5 – ensure `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')` on error and remove it on success.

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters change, the product count badge text is updated but no aria‑live region is used, so screen‑reader users are not informed of the new count.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a hidden live region (e.g., `
`) and update its textContent each time `_updateCountBadge` runs.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the mobile navigation drawer changes visibility but no live‑region announcement is made, leaving AT users unaware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened')`; after closing, announce “Navigation menu closed”.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play/pause button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel’s play/pause toggle changes its visual state but never updates `aria-pressed`, so AT cannot tell whether autoplay is active.
issuesFound.fix: In `togglePlay`, after toggling, call `setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', String(_playing))`.

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField drag‑and‑drop zone does not set aria‑describedby or aria‑label
issuesFound.explanation: The upload zone is focusable but lacks an accessible name or description, making its purpose unclear to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_zoneEl, 'role', 'button')`, `setAttr(_zoneEl, 'aria-label', 'Upload files – click or drag files here')`, and optionally `setAttr(_zoneEl, 'aria-describedby', <id‑of‑status‑element>)`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown registration (`register(trigger, panel)`) adds `aria-haspopup` and `aria-controls` to the trigger but never sets `aria-expanded`. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: In `register()` after setting `aria-controls` add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` and update it in `_openInstance`/`_closeInstance` (e.g. `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and `'false'` when closing).

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav toggle missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) is used to open/close the off‑canvas drawer but never receives an `aria-controls` reference to the drawer element, violating the “toggle trigger missing aria‑controls” rule.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.init()` after locating `_trigger` and `_drawer`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'));`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle (`_toggleEl`) is a button that switches between month and year but never updates `aria-pressed`, leaving its state ambiguous to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In `pricing.init()` after locating `_toggleEl`, add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', 'false');` and in `_onToggleClick()` toggle it with `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(!_toggleEl.getAttribute('aria-pressed') === 'true'));`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle button does not reference the element that displays the price, so screen readers cannot associate the control with its target.
issuesFound.fix: In `pricing.init()` after locating `_toggleEl` and the price container (e.g., the first element with `[data-price-display]`), add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(priceContainer, 'price-display'));`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate()` calls `srUtils.markValid()` which removes error text but never removes the native `aria-invalid` attribute, leaving the field permanently marked as invalid for assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: In `srUtils.markValid()` add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` after removing describedby.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on error fields
issuesFound.explanation: `srUtils.markInvalid()` only adds describedby but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid input, so AT does not recognise the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In `srUtils.markInvalid()` add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` before calling `addDescribedBy`.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateCountBadge()` updates the visual count but does not announce the new total to screen readers, violating dynamic‑content‑announcement guidelines.
issuesFound.fix: After setting the badge text, add `liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion does not update aria‑expanded on open/close
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open(item)` and `.close(item)` toggle visibility but never set the trigger’s `aria-expanded` attribute, leaving the state hidden from AT.
issuesFound.fix: In `open(item)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in `close(item)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu does not set aria-expanded on trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button’s expanded state is never reflected via `aria-expanded`, causing a mismatch between visual and AT state.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance(inst)` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in `_closeInstance(inst)` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav toggle does not set aria-expanded when opened/closed via code
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle button’s `aria-expanded` value is never updated when the drawer opens or closes, so AT cannot track state changes.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.open()` add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in `mobileNav.close()` add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce new price via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, the UI updates price numbers but does not announce the change, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the new cost.
issuesFound.fix: At the end of `setPeriod()` after updating all plan cards, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Pricing updated for selected billing period');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not set aria‑expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav‑toggle button that opens and closes the mobile navigation drawer never receives an aria‑expanded attribute, so screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true) and setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false) respectively.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls link to the drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav‑toggle button does not have an aria‑controls attribute that points to the #nav‑menu element, breaking the relationship between control and controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: After locating the drawer element, add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id) during mobileNav.init.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden state not reflected with aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed, the code only adds a CSS class and sets hidden on the overlay, but never sets aria‑hidden="true" on the drawer element, leaving it in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.close add setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, "true") and in mobileNav.open remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panels are hidden with hidden attribute but lack aria‑hidden updates
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is closed the code sets hidden but does not set aria‑hidden="true", and when opened it removes hidden without removing aria‑hidden, leaving the panel incorrectly exposed to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance add removeAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN) and in _ closeInstance add setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, "true").

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button missing aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The element [data-billing-toggle] acts as a binary switch (monthly ↔ annual) but never receives an aria‑pressed attribute, so screen‑reader users cannot tell its current state.
issuesFound.fix: In pricing._onToggleClick and pricing._onPeriodBtnClick add setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, isYear ? "true" : "false") and update on each change.

issuesFound.title: Inline field validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid inputs
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator adds error messages and CSS classes but never sets aria‑invalid="true" on the field, nor removes it on valid input, breaking the required ARIA mapping for form errors.
issuesFound.fix: In _inlineValidator._renderResult when result.valid is false call setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, "true") and when true call removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID).

issuesFound.title: Contact form email field validation does not reflect aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The email <input> is validated via inlineValidator but the resulting error is only shown visually; aria‑invalid remains unset, so AT cannot detect the error.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the email field receives setAttr(emailEl, ARIA.INVALID, "true") on validation failure and removeAttr(emailEl, ARIA.INVALID) on success.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form submit button does not announce success via live region
issuesFound.explanation: After a successful subscription the code updates a status <div> but the element lacks role="status" or aria‑live, so the success message may not be announced.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_statusEl, "role", "status") and setAttr(_statusEl, "aria-live", "polite") when creating _statusEl.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close actions are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the mobile menu triggers visual changes and emits custom events, but no live‑region announcement is made, leaving users unaware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: After mobileNav.open call liveRegion.announce("Navigation menu opened") and after mobileNav.close call liveRegion.announce("Navigation menu closed").

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions lack live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Users cannot hear when a dropdown panel opens or closes because no aria‑live update occurs.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge updates are not announced via live region

issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which keeps the content visible in the layout rather than hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This defeats the purpose of the utility.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons include transform in their transition property, which can cause motion sensitivity issues. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this animation for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero CTA button
issuesFound.explanation: The hero primary button includes transform transitions without a reduced-motion override. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from the translateY transform animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on featured pricing card
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card hover state applies transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01) without a reduced-motion override. This spatial movement animation is not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words. Negative word-spacing can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for secondary text
issuesFound.explanation: The --colour-text-secondary custom property is set to #aaaaaa. When used against the white background (#ffffff), this creates a contrast ratio of approximately 2.2:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.

  --colour-text-secondary:   #595959;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for neutral-400 text
issuesFound.explanation: The --colour-neutral-400 value (#94a3b8) against white backgrounds creates a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1, failing the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text. This is used for placeholder text and labels.

Line 32:
  --colour-neutral-400:  #5a6b7d;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for error colour
issuesFound.explanation: The --colour-error value (#ee9999) against white backgrounds creates a contrast ratio of approximately 2.0:1, failing the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1. This red may be used for error states and required markers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 40:
  --colour-error:         #d32f2f;

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions not updating aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The functions that open and close dropdown panels (_openInstance and _closeInstance) modify the panel's hidden attribute and CSS classes but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the expanded or collapsed state. Screen readers cannot announce the current state of the dropdown to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`
  Line (in _closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions not updating aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer open and close functions toggle CSS classes and the hidden attribute on the drawer and overlay elements, but fail to update aria-expanded on the hamburger trigger button (_trigger). This prevents assistive technologies from announcing whether the navigation is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in open function): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `_open = true;`
  Line (in close function): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item, the function removes the hidden attribute from the panel and adds CSS classes, but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. While the close function sets aria-expanded="false", the mismatch means the expanded state is never communicated to assistive technologies when opening.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in open function): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a new dropdown pair, the code sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls on the trigger, but does not initialise aria-expanded="false". This means screen readers encounter the trigger without knowing it controls an initially collapsed panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in register function, after setting aria-controls): Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: During initialisation, the mobile navigation trigger button is discovered and event listeners attached, but the code does not set aria-expanded="false" to indicate the drawer is initially closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in init function, after _initialised = true;): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is configured as a combobox with aria-controls pointing to the suggestion list, but aria-expanded is not initialised to "false" when the page loads and the list is hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in mount function, after setAttr(input, 'aria-controls', _listId);): Add `setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panels not hidden from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When closing a dropdown, the panel is hidden using the hidden attribute and CSS classes, but aria-hidden="true" is not set. Although the hidden attribute removes the element from the accessibility tree in modern browsers, explicit aria-hidden ensures consistent behavior across older assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in _closeInstance function, after inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');): Add `setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion panels not hidden from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ panel is hidden via the hidden attribute in the animation callback, but aria-hidden="true" is not set to explicitly remove it from the accessibility tree during the closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in close function's onComplete callback): Add `setAttr(item.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');` alongside `item.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not hidden from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When closing the mobile navigation, the drawer has its open class removed and the overlay is hidden, but aria-hidden="true" is not set on the drawer element to explicitly hide it from assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in close function): Add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');` when closing, and correspondingly `removeAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN);` in the open function.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid on fields
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, the function adds error classes and updates aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field. When validation passes, it does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute. Screen readers cannot convey the valid/invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _renderResult, valid block): Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`
  Line (in _renderResult, invalid block): Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the prices update visually with animation, but the change in billing period and prices is not announced to screen reader users via the live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in setPeriod, after _syncToggleState): Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer changes the visible state significantly, but no announcement is made to inform screen reader users that the navigation has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in open function): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`
  Line (in close function): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus open or close, the visual change is not accompanied by a live region announcement, leaving screen reader users unaware that a menu has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _openInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');`
  Line (in _closeInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ answers changes the content height and visibility, but the state change is not announced via the live region, so screen reader users may not realize the answer has expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in open function): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened ? STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question) : `${item.question} expanded`);`
  Line (in close function): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed ? STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question) : `${item.question} collapsed`);`

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the scroll-to-top button is activated, the page scrolls to the top and focus is moved, but no announcement confirms the action was successful. Users may not understand what happened if they did not perceive the scroll.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in _onClick function): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page');` after `scrollToY(0);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger menu button that controls the mobile navigation drawer does not have its aria-expanded attribute updated when the drawer opens or closes. Screen readers cannot announce the state change to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button lacks an aria-controls attribute linking it to the drawer element it controls. This prevents screen reader users from understanding which element is being expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open does not move focus to drawer content
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, keyboard focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving to the drawer content. Keyboard users cannot immediately access the navigation links without tabbing through the entire page.
issuesFound.fix: Line (after addClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen)): `const firstLink = qs('a', _drawer) || _drawer; if (firstLink) firstLink.focus();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap not activated and focus not returned on close
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer does not trap keyboard focus when open, allowing users to tab out of the drawer into the background content. Additionally, when closed, focus is not returned to the trigger button. The _trap variable exists but is never instantiated with createFocusTrap.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in open function after _lockScroll()): `if (!_trap) _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); if (_trap) _trap.activate();`
  Line (in close function at start): `if (_trap && _trap.isActive()) _trap.deactivate();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance/_closeInstance do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu trigger buttons do not have their aria-expanded attribute updated when panels open or close. Screen readers cannot announce whether the dropdown is expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.fix: Line (in _closeInstance before emit): `if (returnFocus && inst.trigger) inst.trigger.focus();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu non-button triggers lack Enter/Space keyboard handlers
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown triggers are not native button elements, the code sets role="button" and tabindex="0" but does not add keyboard event listeners for Enter and Space keys. Keyboard users cannot activate these dropdowns.
issuesFound.fix: Line (after wrapper.addEventListener('mouseleave', ...)): `trigger.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); _onTriggerClick.call(trigger, e); } });`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close functions do not update aria-expanded

Line (in open function after item.open = true): `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line (in close function after item.open = false): `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion non-button triggers lack Enter/Space keyboard handlers
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ triggers are not native button elements, the code sets role="button" and tabindex="0" but does not add keyboard event listeners for Enter and Space keys. Keyboard users cannot activate these accordions.
